name: French Onion, Garlic and Cumin Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 Onion, peeled, halved and sliced 1/8 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large frying pan or skillet over medium heat, cook the bacon, onion, and garlic until lightly browned and the bacon is crispy. Stir in the pecans, salt, pepper, rosemary, and cumin seed. Set aside.
2. In the work bowl of a food processor using the plastic dough blade, combine 3 cups of bread flour and the sauteed onion mixture. Add the yeast, egg, and Swiss cheese and combine thoroughly. With the machine still running, add the water slowly until the mixture begins to pull together and forms a ball. If the dough is sticky, add the remaining flour 1/4 cup at a time. Process until the dough cleans the sides of the work bowl, then knead for approximately 1 minute longer.
3. Place the dough into a lightly oiled freezer-type plastic bag and put in a warm place to double in volume, about 1 hour.
4. Remove the dough from the bag and punch down. Shape the dough into a round, placing the dough on a lightly greased baking sheet that has been dusted with cornmeal. Make 3 or 4 decorative slashes into the top of the bread and brush with the egg glaze. Allow to rise again in a warm, draft-free area until doubled, about 45 minutes.
5. Place the dough into a preheated 375°F (190°C) oven and cook for 35-40 minutes or until the bread is golden and the bottom sounds hollow when tapped with your finger (or the dough registers 190 degrees on an instant-read thermometer).
6. Place the bread directly on a wire rack to cool. This bread freezes well for 2-3 months if tightly wrapped. To serve, defrost overnight in foil. To reheat, place the thawed bread, still wrapped in foil, in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 15-20 minutes. If reheated this way, the bread will retain its freshly baked qualities.
